facts.text: Wildwood Crest was founded in 1895 by the Wildwood Beach Improvement Company, naming the borough after its forested seaside setting.
The borough’s name comes from the wild flowers that once carpeted the shoreline, giving it a natural, botanical charm.
Wildwood Crest’s history spans just over a century, yet it has witnessed more than a dozen memorable events since its 1910 incorporation.
NJ Transit’s 313, 315, 319, and 552 buses connect Wildwood Crest to Cape May, Philadelphia, Manhattan, and Atlantic City, keeping the borough linked to major hubs.
The historic Hereford Inlet Lighthouse, perched on a rocky spit, stands as Wildwood Crest’s oldest maritime landmark.
Wildwood Crest is one of only five New Jersey municipalities that offer free, lifeguard‑monitored public oceanfront beaches, a rare perk for coastal towns.
